paper_title: Multiple strain analysis of Streptomyces species from Philippine marine sediments reveals intraspecies heterogeneity in antibiotic activities

methods.description: Collection of marine sediment samples from geographically remote sites in west-central Philippines to study actinomycetes.

steps.description: Marine sediment collection was conducted from 2017 to 2018, specifically between March and June.
steps.searchable_summary: collect marine sediment samples using SCUBA diving

steps.description: The 110cm compact sediment samples were partitioned into five layers at 25-cm increments from the seabed.
The salinity and pH of seawater were measured using a refractometer and pH meter, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: measure salinity and pH of seawater

steps.description: Samples in tubes were secured in a ziplock container, maintained temporarily on ice, and transported immediately to the laboratory for storage at 4 °C until further analysis.

methods.description: Isolation of actinomycetes from marine sediments using dry stamp and heat shock methods to eliminate undesirable microorganisms.

steps.description: Marine sediments were processed in the laboratory using dry stamp (DSM) and heat shock method (HSM).
steps.searchable_summary: process sediments using DSM and HSM

steps.description: Sediments were inoculated in different minimal marine media containing various carbon sources.
After 30 to 60 days of incubation, the growth of actinomycete was recognized by the presence of filamentous hyphae and/or by the formation of tough, leathery colonies.
Actinomycete spores in minimal marine media were transferred to enriched marine medium 1 (MM1) and incubated to obtain pure culture of isolates.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer spores to enriched medium and incubate

methods.method_name: Resazurin Agar Overlay Assay for Antibacterial Screening
methods.description: Screening actinomycete strains for antibacterial activities against test pathogens using a resazurin reduction test.

steps.description: 14-day old pure actinomycete isolate was inoculated using a sterile toothpick in MM1 agar molded in a well of a 96-well microtiter plate.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate actinomycete isolate in microtiter plate

steps.description: After seven days of incubation, each isolate was overlaid with bacterial suspension in trypticase soy agar.
steps.searchable_summary: overlay bacterial suspension on incubated isolates

steps.description: After incubation, resazurin dye was added to each well, and wells that retained the blue color indicated antibacterial activity.
steps.searchable_summary: add resazurin dye and assess color change
